petitioner_background: The petitioner is a self-represented individual who filed an employment-based immigrant visa petition seeking classification as an alien of extraordinary ability under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act. The decision does not specify the petitioner's field of work, nationality, or career details. The petition was filed at the Vermont Service Center (EAC 05 011 50034).
outcome_reasoning: The AAO summarily dismissed the appeal because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v). The appellate submission was unaccompanied by arguments or evidence addressing the pertinent regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3), and the petitioner indicated she was not submitting a separate brief or evidence. The petitioner's appeal statement merely asserted that her works had gained a recognized reputation and requested reconsideration, without addressing the reasons stated for denial or providing additional evidence.
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural issue: The appeal was summarily dismissed under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact. The petitioner did not submit a brief, arguments, or additional evidence addressing the regulatory criteria.
